messages.text: Basket 1 is a light tan woven wicker basket shaped like a mouse/rodent—pointed snout with a little dark nose and small bead eyes—plus a flat strap-like handle running along the top/back.
Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-shaped wicker basket with a very dark brown thick arched handle, and a distinctive wooden-looking band around the top rim with little metal studs/tacks.
Basket 3: rectangular picnic basket with a tall center handle and a hinged lid that’s open/flipped up to the right side.
Small oval/boat-shaped wicker basket with one arched handle that has light/white wrapping bands near the top; pale body with a darker brown rim/accents.
Is it the two-tone basket with a pale middle band and darker top/bottom?
Basket 5: a medium-brown, boxy rectangular wicker basket with a very thick twisted-rope style handle arching high; sides have a tight checker weave.
Basket 6: the round lidded wicker basket shaped like a cat—cat face on top (eyes/whiskers) and little feet at the bottom.
Basket 7: the boxy rectangular basket with green/teal woven sides and an orange-brown rim/handle (braided band along the top edge).
dark gray/black oval basket with a single dark handle, and a contrasting light tan band around the bottom edge
Basket 9: tall cylindrical brown wicker basket, open top, with two small loop handles on opposite sides near the rim (no big arch handle).
Basket 10: a very shallow, round tray-like wicker basket with a flat spiral-woven base and a single arch handle; light tan with a few reddish bands.

Basket 11: a deep round bucket/tub basket with a thick rolled rim and two small loop handles on the sides (no big arch handle); brown wicker with a diagonal/chevron weave and darker banding near the bottom.
Basket 12: small light-brown oval basket with one arch handle and a distinctive wavy/scalloped dip in the front rim; open lattice sides with vertical slats.

distinctive_features: has a pointed snout with a small dark nose
two small bead-like eyes (one looks bluish)
a flat handle/strap running along the top/back
reasoning.discriminative_strategy: Emphasize the animal (mouse) shape and the top strap handle; distinguish from the round cat-face basket.
